proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin (`albumin_g_dl`) is associated with longer progression-free survival (`pfs_months`); the OLS slope of pfs_months on albumin_g_dl is positive.
Worse performance status (higher `ecog_ps`) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; the OLS slope is negative.
Patients with `stage_iv` = 1 have shorter mean `pfs_months` than those with `stage_iv` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs ~ albumin: beta=0.465 per g/dL, p=3.25e-115
OLS pfs ~ ecog_ps: beta=-1.190 months per ECOG point, p=0.00e+00
Mean PFS stage_iv=1: 3.703 vs stage_iv=0: 5.055 (t-test p=0.00e+00)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher tumor burden marker CEA (log1p(`cea_ng_ml`)) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
Higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
Higher `nlr` (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
Higher `crp_mg_l` (inflammation) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
Greater 6-month weight loss (`weight_loss_pct_6mo`) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs ~ LDH: beta=-0.0003 per U/L, p=1.26e-02
OLS pfs ~ CRP: beta=-0.0020 per mg/L, p=6.50e-02
OLS pfs ~ weight loss %: beta=-0.0730, p=2.21e-167

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (`age_years`) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
Mean `pfs_months` differs between female (`sex_female`=1) and male patients.
Patients with `right_sided_primary`=1 have shorter mean `pfs_months` than those with left-sided primary.

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs ~ age: beta=0.1765 per year, p=0.00e+00
Mean PFS female: 4.308 vs male: 4.314 (t-test p=7.70e-01)
Mean PFS right-sided: 4.114 vs left-sided: 4.419 (t-test p=5.23e-46)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with cetuximab (`treatment_cetuximab`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
Treatment with bevacizumab (`treatment_bevacizumab`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
Treatment with pembrolizumab (`treatment_pembrolizumab`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
Treatment with encorafenib (`treatment_encorafenib`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
Treatment with trastuzumab/tucatinib (`treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
Treatment with regorafenib (`treatment_regorafenib`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.

Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is restricted to KRAS wild-type patients: in `kras_mutation`=0 the cetuximab-vs-no-cetuximab PFS difference is positive, and the treatment_cetuximab×kras_mutation interaction is negative.
In `kras_mutation`=1 patients, cetuximab does not improve `pfs_months` (effect ~ 0).

analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS=0: cetuximab effect = -0.048 months (n_on=8821, p=1.24e-01)
Within KRAS=1: cetuximab effect = -0.028 months (n_on=6257, p=3.76e-01)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is restricted to NRAS wild-type patients (interaction with `nras_mutation` negative).
Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is restricted to BRAF wild-type patients (interaction with `braf_v600e` negative).
Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is greater in left-sided primary (`right_sided_primary`=0) than right-sided.
In RAS/RAF wild-type patients (`kras_mutation`=0 AND `nras_mutation`=0 AND `braf_v600e`=0), cetuximab is associated with longer `pfs_months` than no cetuximab.
In RAS/RAF wild-type AND left-sided (`right_sided_primary`=0) patients, cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is even larger than in the broader RAS/RAF WT group.

analyses.result_summary: In RAS/RAF wild-type (n=25324): cetuximab PFS effect = -0.045 months (p=1.74e-01)
In RAS/RAF WT, left-sided (n=16443): cetuximab PFS effect = -0.067 months (p=1.29e-01)
Outside RAS/RAF-WT-left (n=33557): cetuximab PFS effect = -0.032 months (p=2.07e-01)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on `pfs_months` is restricted to MSI-high (`msi_high`=1) patients; the treatment_pembrolizumab×msi_high interaction is positive.

Encorafenib benefit on `pfs_months` is concentrated in BRAF V600E (`braf_v600e`=1) patients; the treatment_encorafenib×braf_v600e interaction is positive (i.e., encorafenib effect more positive in BRAF mut).
Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit on `pfs_months` is concentrated in HER2-amplified (`her2_amplified`=1) patients; the treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib×her2_amplified interaction is positive.
Within HER2-amplified, the trastuzumab/tucatinib effect on `pfs_months` is greater when patients are also RAS wild-type (`kras_mutation`=0 AND `nras_mutation`=0).

analyses.result_summary: In HER2+ AND KRAS WT AND NRAS WT (n=845): trastuzumab/tucatinib PFS effect = +0.052 months (p=8.63e-01)
In HER2+ AND (KRAS or NRAS mut) (n=659): trastuzumab/tucatinib PFS effect = -0.089 months (p=7.82e-01)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Bevacizumab effect on `pfs_months` differs by KRAS mutation status (treatment_bevacizumab×kras_mutation interaction is non-zero).
Bevacizumab effect on `pfs_months` differs by tumor sidedness (treatment_bevacizumab×right_sided_primary interaction is non-zero).
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by KRAS mutation status (interaction non-zero).
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by tumor sidedness (interaction non-zero).
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by BRAF V600E status (interaction non-zero).
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by MSI-high status (interaction non-zero).
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by stage_iv status (interaction non-zero).
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by HER2 amplification (interaction non-zero).
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by ECOG performance status.
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by age.
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by log(CEA); higher CEA reduces benefit (interaction negative).
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by albumin.
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by LDH.
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by NLR.
After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, stage_iv, sidedness, RAS/RAF/MSI/HER2 biomarkers, and labs, the adjusted association of cetuximab with `pfs_months` is positive.
After multivariable adjustment, bevacizumab is associated with longer `pfs_months`.
After multivariable adjustment, pembrolizumab is associated with longer `pfs_months`.
After multivariable adjustment, encorafenib is associated with longer `pfs_months`.
After multivariable adjustment, trastuzumab/tucatinib is associated with longer `pfs_months`.
After multivariable adjustment, regorafenib remains associated with longer `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS effect of treatment_cetuximab on PFS: beta=-0.010 months, p=2.29e-01
Adjusted OLS effect of treatment_bevacizumab on PFS: beta=0.002 months, p=7.71e-01
Adjusted OLS effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on PFS: beta=-0.000 months, p=9.67e-01
Adjusted OLS effect of treatment_encorafenib on PFS: beta=0.007 months, p=5.98e-01
Adjusted OLS effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on PFS: beta=0.026 months, p=6.45e-02
Adjusted OLS effect of treatment_regorafenib on PFS: beta=0.942 months, p=0.00e+00

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive treatment-by-feature interaction screen across {age, sex, ECOG, stage_iv, sidedness, KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, MSI, HER2, NTRK, log_cea, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin} will identify at least one feature whose interaction with cetuximab is significant at p<0.05.
The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of bevacizumab effect on `pfs_months`.
The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of pembrolizumab effect on `pfs_months`.
The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of encorafenib effect on `pfs_months`.
The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of trastuzumab/tucatinib effect on `pfs_months`.
The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of regorafenib effect on `pfs_months`; the strongest will involve KRAS.

analyses.result_summary: Top 3 modifier candidates for treatment_cetuximab: msi_high: beta=+0.162 p=1.15e-01; ecog_ps: beta=+0.035 p=2.54e-01; age_years: beta=-0.002 p=2.69e-01
Top 3 modifier candidates for treatment_bevacizumab: braf_v600e: beta=-0.228 p=2.22e-02; hemoglobin_g_dl: beta=+0.023 p=4.19e-02; weight_loss_pct_6mo: beta=-0.008 p=1.10e-01
Top 3 modifier candidates for treatment_pembrolizumab: crp_mg_l: beta=-0.007 p=2.05e-02; right_sided_primary: beta=-0.116 p=4.98e-02; nras_mutation: beta=-0.285 p=9.35e-02
Top 3 modifier candidates for treatment_encorafenib: msi_high: beta=-0.296 p=6.16e-02; nlr: beta=-0.026 p=1.10e-01; ldh_u_l: beta=-0.001 p=2.34e-01
Top 3 modifier candidates for treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib: crp_mg_l: beta=+0.009 p=5.44e-02; weight_loss_pct_6mo: beta=-0.015 p=1.12e-01; right_sided_primary: beta=+0.096 p=2.19e-01
Top 3 modifier candidates for treatment_regorafenib: kras_mutation: beta=-1.629 p=7.69e-226; log_cea: beta=-0.758 p=1.02e-175; right_sided_primary: beta=-1.454 p=4.08e-168

proposed_hypotheses.text: Best subgroup for cetuximab benefit: KRAS WT AND NRAS WT AND BRAF WT AND left-sided primary; in this subgroup cetuximab improves `pfs_months`.
Best subgroup for pembrolizumab benefit: MSI-high; in this subgroup pembrolizumab improves `pfs_months`.
Best subgroup for encorafenib benefit: BRAF V600E mutation; in this subgroup encorafenib improves `pfs_months`.
Best subgroup for trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit: HER2-amplified AND RAS WT; in this subgroup it improves `pfs_months`.
Bevacizumab provides a small adjusted positive effect on `pfs_months` in the overall cohort (no clear subgroup).
Regorafenib provides a positive effect on `pfs_months` in patients with ECOG performance status 0.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab in KRAS WT, NRAS WT, BRAF WT, left-sided (n=16443): PFS effect=-0.067 months, p=1.29e-01
Pembrolizumab in MSI-high (n=2513): PFS effect=+0.007 months, p=9.55e-01
Encorafenib in BRAF V600E (n=2272): PFS effect=-0.129 months, p=3.59e-01
Trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2+ AND RAS WT (n=845): PFS effect=+0.052 months, p=8.63e-01
Adjusted OLS bevacizumab main effect: beta=0.003, p=7.28e-01
Regorafenib in ECOG 0 (n=17592): PFS effect=+0.925 months, p=1.95e-115

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is concentrated specifically in KRAS WT AND left-sided patients; the effect is largest in (kras_mutation=0, right_sided_primary=0) and minimal in the other three cells.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab effect in KRAS=0, right_sided=0 (n=18853): -0.065 months, p=1.13e-01
Cetuximab effect in KRAS=0, right_sided=1 (n=10249): -0.031 months, p=4.92e-01
Cetuximab effect in KRAS=1, right_sided=0 (n=13490): -0.036 months, p=3.60e-01
Cetuximab effect in KRAS=1, right_sided=1 (n=7408): -0.014 months, p=7.94e-01

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within RAS wild-type (KRAS WT and NRAS WT), the cetuximab×BRAF V600E interaction on `pfs_months` is negative — BRAF V600E suppresses cetuximab benefit even after restricting to RAS WT.

analyses.result_summary: In RAS WT: cetuximab*BRAF interaction beta=-0.017, p=8.80e-01

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within BRAF V600E, the encorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is concentrated in KRAS WT (BRAF V600E and KRAS mutation are typically mutually exclusive but may have an effect modifier here).

analyses.result_summary: Encorafenib in BRAF+ AND KRAS=0 (n=2272): -0.129 months, p=3.59e-01
Encorafenib in BRAF+ AND KRAS=1 (n=0): +nan months, p=nan

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab effect on `pfs_months` is modified by sidedness, KRAS, stage_iv, or ECOG (test each).

analyses.result_summary: In MSI-high: pembro*right_sided_primary interaction beta=-0.306, p=2.43e-01
In MSI-high: pembro*kras_mutation interaction beta=-0.161, p=5.25e-01
In MSI-high: pembro*stage_iv interaction beta=-0.186, p=4.42e-01
In MSI-high: pembro*ecog_ps interaction beta=-0.054, p=7.52e-01

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit on `pfs_months` is concentrated in HER2-amplified AND fully RAS/RAF wild-type patients (KRAS WT, NRAS WT, BRAF WT).

analyses.result_summary: Trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2+ AND KRAS WT AND NRAS WT AND BRAF WT (n=761): PFS effect=+0.014 months, p=9.66e-01

proposed_hypotheses.text: Bevacizumab effect on `pfs_months` is modified by log(CEA), albumin, LDH, ECOG, or age (test each interaction).
Regorafenib improves `pfs_months` in KRAS wild-type (`kras_mutation`=0) patients but has no effect (or is null) in KRAS mutant patients.
Mean `pfs_months` differs between patients with `ntrk_fusion`=1 and those without.

analyses.result_summary: Patients with NTRK fusion: n=251; mean PFS=4.351 vs no NTRK=4.311

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, none of the canonical biomarker × treatment interactions (cetuximab×KRAS/NRAS/BRAF/sidedness, pembrolizumab×MSI, encorafenib×BRAF, trastuzumab/tucatinib×HER2) are statistically significant in this dataset (i.e., the dataset's only strong heterogeneity is for regorafenib).
Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` requires KRAS WT: in KRAS WT × BRAF WT it is large and positive; KRAS mutation suppresses the effect; BRAF V600E further suppresses it.
Within KRAS WT × BRAF WT, regorafenib benefit is concentrated in left-sided primary patients (sidedness suppresses the effect when right-sided).
Within KRAS WT × BRAF WT × left-sided, regorafenib benefit is further concentrated in patients with low CEA (`cea_ng_ml` < 5 ng/mL); higher CEA suppresses benefit.

FINAL: Regorafenib produces a large positive effect on `pfs_months` (~+5 months) specifically in patients with `kras_mutation`=0 AND `nras_mutation`=0 AND `braf_v600e`=0 AND `right_sided_primary`=0 AND `cea_ng_ml` < 5 ng/mL; outside this subgroup the effect is essentially null. Each of {KRAS mutation, NRAS mutation (weakly), BRAF V600E, right-sided primary, elevated CEA} suppresses the regorafenib treatment effect.

Joint multivariable interaction model confirms negative interactions of regorafenib with `kras_mutation`, `braf_v600e`, `right_sided_primary`, and `log_cea`, while NRAS mutation is not an independent suppressor.

analyses.result_summary: Rego in KRAS=0, BRAF=0 (n_on=5348,n_off=21482): +1.814 months, p=0.00e+00
Rego in KRAS=0, BRAF=1 (n_on=468,n_off=1804): -0.156 months, p=1.47e-01
Rego in KRAS=1, BRAF=0 (n_on=4206,n_off=16692): +0.027 months, p=4.54e-01
Rego in KRAS=0, BRAF=0, right=0 (n_on=3461,n_off=13911): +2.758 months, p=0.00e+00
Rego in KRAS=0, BRAF=0, right=1 (n_on=1887,n_off=7571): +0.083 months, p=1.20e-01
Rego in KRAS WT, BRAF WT, left-sided, CEA<5 (n_on=1903,n_off=7423): +5.013 months, p=0.00e+00
Rego in KRAS WT, BRAF WT, left-sided, CEA>=5 (n_on=1558,n_off=6488): +0.005 months, p=9.28e-01
Rego in KRAS WT + NRAS WT + BRAF WT + left-sided + CEA<5 (n_on=1790,n_off=7041): +5.000 months, p=0.00e+00
Rego in COMPLEMENT (any unfavorable feature) (n_on=8232,n_off=32937): +0.096 months, p=1.93e-04
Multi-interaction model regorafenib main effect: beta=+3.613, p=0.00e+00
